title: 10 best herbal teas for oral thrust
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oral thrush are natural remedies made from plants and herbs that can help alleviate the symptoms of oral thrush, a fungal infection that occurs in the mouth. These teas typically contain antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation, fight off the Candida fungus, and soothe irritation in the mouth. Examples of herbal teas that can help with oral thrush include chamomile, calendula, echinacea, and goldenseal. By drinking these teas, individuals can experience relief from symptoms such as white patches, soreness, and difficulty swallowing, ultimately improving their quality of life by allowing them to eat and speak comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with oral thrush because it contains antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help to eliminate the Candida fungus responsible for the infection. The active compounds in sage, such as salvianolic acids and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the most common cause of oral thrush. Additionally, sage tea can help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort associated with oral thrush. Drinking sage tea or using it as a mouthwash can help to alleviate the symptoms of oral thrush and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage tea contains compounds such as camphor, eucalyptol, and rosmarinic acid, which have antimicrobial effects that can help reduce the growth of bacteria and viruses responsible for oral thrush.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea has anti-inflammatory agents like carnosic acid and carnosol, which can help alleviate inflammation and redness in the mouth caused by oral thrush.

Antifungal properties: Sage tea contains compounds like thujone and 1,8-cineole, which have antifungal effects that can help inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.

Astringent properties: Sage tea contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce the inflammation and redness associated with oral thrush, as well as promote healing of the affected tissues.

Immune-boosting properties: Sage tea is rich in antioxidants and vitamins, such as vitamin C and A, that can help boost the immune system and aid in the body's ability to fight off the infection caused by oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried sage leaves to a tea infuser or teapot for 3-5 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the sage leaves and let it steep for 3-5 minutes.
Remove the sage leaves and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the sage tea slowly, allowing it to coat the mouth and throat as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves are commonly used in sage teas to help alleviate oral thrush symptoms.
Flowers: Containing antifungal and antioxidant properties, sage flowers can help combat the Candida fungus causing oral thrush.
Roots: Packed with therapeutic constituents, sage roots can be used in teas to soothe inflammation and fight off oral thrush-causing infections.
Bark: With astringent properties, the bark of sage plants can help reduce irritation and promote healing in oral thrush cases when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with oral thrush because it contains antimicrobial properties that can inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. The menthol present in peppermint tea has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help soothe and relieve pain and inflammation in the mouth and throat. Additionally, peppermint tea can help freshen breath and promote overall oral health. Drinking peppermint tea regularly can help prevent the recurrence of oral thrush and maintain a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Peppermint tea contains menthol and other compounds that can help inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oral thrush.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint tea can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort in the mouth and throat caused by oral thrush.
Analgesic: Peppermint tea has natural pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush.
Immune-boosting: Peppermint tea is rich in antioxidants and other compounds that can help support the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections like oral thrush.
Antifungal: Peppermint tea contains compounds like menthol and carvacrol that have been shown to have antifungal properties, making it effective in treating oral thrush caused by Candida fungus.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 cup of boiling water with 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 5-10 minutes to allow the beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the spent peppermint leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Sip the herbal peppermint tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to soothe oral thrush symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for oral thrush due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers are used in teas for oral thrush as they contain flavonoids and essential oils that have antimicrobial and soothing effects.
Seeds: The seeds of the peppermint plant are used in tea preparations for oral thrush because they are rich in menthol, which has analgesic and antiseptic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with oral thrush because it possesses antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of this fungal infection. The compound citral, found in lemon balm, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and soothe painful, irritated tissues in the mouth. Drinking lemon balm tea can provide relief from the discomfort and help speed up the healing process.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity of oral thrush by inhibiting the growth of Candida fungus.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to its eugenol content, which can help reduce inflammation and redness in the mouth caused by oral thrush.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm tea contains flavonoids, which act as antioxidants, helping to protect the oral tissues from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, common in oral thrush.
Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid and citral, which have anxiolytic effects, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, contributing factors to oral thrush.

Immune-boosting property: Lemon balm tea has immune-boosting properties due to its flavonoids and polyphenols, which can help strengthen the immune system and improve the body's ability to fight off infections, including oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its healing properties.
Strain the tea, discard the leaves, and drink it while still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for oral thrush due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe inflamed mucous membranes in the mouth.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and essential oils, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal effects, making them useful in treating oral thrush.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm can still contribute to the medicinal properties of teas for oral thrush, as they contain similar compounds.

Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are less commonly used in teas for oral thrush, but they can still provide some benefits due to their essential oil content, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with oral thrush because it has powerful antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in fighting the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush. The herb's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, can help reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and promote healing in the affected areas. Moreover, thyme tea is a natural and gentle alternative to conventional antifungal medications, making it a suitable option for individuals seeking a more holistic approach to managing oral thrush. Regular consumption of thyme tea can help alleviate symptoms, support oral health, and maintain a balanced oral microbiome.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for oral thrush, such as Candida albicans. This is due to the presence of active compounds like thymol and carvacrol.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate inflammation and redness in the mouth caused by oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids.

Antifungal: Thyme tea exhibits antifungal properties that can help combat the overgrowth of Candida fungus in the mouth. The primary active compounds responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.

Immune-boosting: Thyme tea can help strengthen the immune system, which is crucial for fighting off infections like oral thrush. The immune-boosting properties of thyme tea can be attributed to its high antioxidant content, including vitamin C and various polyphenols.

Analgesic: Thyme tea has analgesic properties that can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils like thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the thyme leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's medicinal properties.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for oral thrush treatment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in thymol and carvacrol, which have antiseptic properties that can help to treat oral thrush.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating oral thrush.
Stems: Thyme stems contain essential oils that have antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with oral thrush because it contains powerful antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to eliminate the Candida fungus that causes oral thrush. The oregano herb contains compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans. Drinking oregano tea can help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush. Additionally, oregano tea can help to boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off the infection and prevent it from recurring.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Oregano tea contains carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with oral thrush.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Oregano tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, that can help reduce swelling and redness in the mouth caused by oral thrush.
Antioxidant properties: Oregano tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the mouth's tissues from damage caused by the Candida fungus.
Immune-boosting properties: Oregano tea has immune-boosting compounds, such as ursolic acid, that can help the body fight off the Candida fungus and prevent the recurrence of oral thrush.
Analgesic properties: Oregano tea contains compounds, such as linalool, that have pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried oregano leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the oregano leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the oregano leaves.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for oral thrush relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for oral thrush due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano plant contain potent antimicrobial compounds, making them useful for treating oral thrush.
Seeds: Oregano seeds are rich in thymol, a compound with antiseptic and fungicidal properties, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas for oral thrush.
Stems: The stems of the oregano plant contain essential oils with antifungal and antimicrobial properties, making them suitable for use in medicinal teas for oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Winter savory tea helps with oral thrust because it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria that cause oral thrust. The tea is made from the leaves of the winter savory plant, which contains compounds such as carvacrol and thymol that have been shown to have antimicrobial effects. Drinking winter savory tea may help to soothe irritation and promote healing in the mouth, making it a natural and effective remedy for oral thrust. Additionally, winter savory tea is caffeine-free and has a mild, pleasant taste, making it a comfortable choice for people of all ages.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Winter savory tea has the ability to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria, including those that cause oral thrush, due to its active constituents such as carvacrol and thymol.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and redness in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort caused by oral thrush, as it contains compounds such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antifungal: Winter savory tea has been found to have antifungal effects, which can help control the growth of Candida fungus, the primary cause of oral thrush, due to the presence of active compounds like carvacrol and thymol.
Immune-boosting: By strengthening the immune system, winter savory tea can help the body fight off infections, including oral thrush, as it contains antioxidants and vitamins like vitamin C.

Wound-healing: Winter savory tea promotes wound healing, which can help the mouth recover from the sores and lesions caused by oral thrush, thanks to its active constituents like rosmarinic acid and tannins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried winter savory leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used herbs.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste for added flavor and potential soothing effects.
Drink the warm tea slowly, allowing it to coat your mouth and throat as you sip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, leaves are commonly used in teas to treat oral thrush due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Flowers: Containing high amounts of antioxidants, flowers are used in medicinal teas to help soothe inflammation and support immune function in cases of oral thrush.
Seeds: Packed with vitamins and minerals, seeds can be used in teas to boost the overall effectiveness of the treatment, providing additional nutritional support for those suffering from oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with oral thrush because it has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can help to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help to soothe inflammation and irritation in the mouth. Moreover, rosemary tea is a natural remedy that can provide relief from oral thrush symptoms without causing any adverse side effects. Drinking rosemary tea can help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Rosemary tea has antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, which can contribute to oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as camphor, cineole, and borneol.

Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth, which are common symptoms of oral thrush. The active constituents that contribute to this property include rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Immune-boosting: Rosemary tea can help boost the immune system, which is important for fighting off infections like oral thrush. The active constituents that contribute to this property include vitamin C, vitamin B6, and iron.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can weaken the immune system and make it more susceptible to infections like oral thrush. The active constituents that contribute to this property include carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid.

Pain-relieving: Rosemary tea has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with oral thrush. The active constituents that contribute to this property include volatile oils such as camphor and cineole.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for every 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves, taking approximately 5-10 minutes to reach a rolling boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used rosemary leaves.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, until symptoms of oral thrush improve.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of rosemary are commonly used in teas for oral thrush due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to help soothe inflammation in the mouth caused by oral thrush.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of rosemary can be included in teas for oral thrush, as they contain compounds with potential antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena tea helps with oral thrush because it contains powerful antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to combat the candida fungus responsible for oral thrush. The tea's soothing and calming effects can also help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with the condition. Moreover, verbena tea is a natural source of antioxidants that can help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. Drinking verbena tea regularly can help to prevent the recurrence of oral thrush and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Verbena tea has the ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, including those that cause oral thrush, due to its active constituents such as verbascoside and luteolin.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the affected area, as it contains compounds like ursolic acid and flavonoids.
Immune-boosting: Verbena tea can enhance the immune system, aiding the body in fighting off infections, thanks to the presence of vitamin C and other antioxidants.

Antifungal: The antifungal properties of verbena tea help in treating oral thrush by inhibiting the growth of Candida fungus, which is responsible for the infection, due to its active constituents like verbascoside and caffeic acid.
Analgesic: Verbena tea can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by oral thrush, as it contains compounds like verbenalin and citral, which have pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried verbena leaves.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Steep the verbena leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the leaves and let the tea cool to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the verbena tea slowly, allowing it to coat the mouth and throat.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial properties, verbena flowers are commonly used to soothe inflammation and fight oral thrush infections.
Leaves: High in tannins and essential oils, verbena leaves are often used in teas to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from the discomfort caused by oral thrush.
Aerial parts: Not included as they are not used.
Roots: Containing active compounds like verbascoside, verbena roots are sometimes used in medicinal teas to help alleviate symptoms of oral thrush and boost the immune system.
Rhizomes: Not used in medicinal teas for oral thrush.
Stems: Not typically utilized in teas for oral thrush due to their lower concentration of beneficial compounds compared to other plant parts.
Seeds: Not used in medicinal teas for oral thrush.
Buds: Not included as they are not used in medicinal teas for oral thrush.
Bark: Not used in medicinal teas for oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with oral thrush because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off the candida fungus responsible for oral thrush. The tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals that can help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. Additionally, dandelion tea is a natural diuretic, which can help flush toxins out of the body and promote overall health. Drinking dandelion tea may provide relief from the symptoms of oral thrush, such as white patches in the mouth and on the tongue, and can help promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tea can reduce inflammation in the mouth, providing relief from the painful symptoms of oral thrush. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in dandelions.

Antimicrobial: Dandelion tea has antimicrobial properties that can help combat the Candida fungus responsible for oral thrush. The active constituents responsible for this effect are sesquiterpene lactones and taraxasterol.

Immune-boosting: Dandelion tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections like oral thrush. The immune-boosting properties come from vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like iron and zinc found in dandelions.

Diuretic: Dandelion tea can help flush out toxins from the body, which can help reduce the severity of oral thrush. The diuretic effect is attributed to the presence of potassium and tannins in dandelions.

Wound healing: Dandelion tea can promote wound healing in the mouth, which can help speed up the recovery process from oral thrush. This property is due to the presence of beta-carotene, vitamin C, and other antioxidants in dandelions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of fresh dandelion flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried dandelion flowers.
Rinse the dandelion flowers thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Place the dandelion flowers in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the dandelion flowers and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain out the dandelion flowers and enjoy the herbal dandelion tea while it's still warm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions are used to make medicinal teas for oral thrush due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to help soothe irritation and inflammation in the mouth caused by oral thrush.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers contain flavonoids and have been used in teas to help reduce inflammation and fight off the fungal infection causing oral thrush.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with oral thrush because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of oral thrush. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush. Additionally, yarrow tea can help soothe inflammation and irritation in the mouth, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Its astringent properties can also help reduce the excess moisture in the mouth that can exacerbate the growth of Candida albicans. By promoting oral health and maintaining a healthy balance of microflora in the mouth, yarrow tea can be a useful natural remedy for oral thrush.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active constituents like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce inflammation associated with oral thrush.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tea, owing to compounds like alpha-pinene and sabinene, inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, the fungus responsible for oral thrush.
Antifungal: Yarrow tea's antifungal effects, attributed to active constituents like chamazulene and azulenes, directly combat the fungal infection causing oral thrush.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, resulting from tannins and essential oils, help to dry and tighten mucous membranes, alleviating symptoms of oral thrush.
Immune-boosting: Yarrow tea's ability to boost the immune system, due to the presence of antioxidants and vitamins, supports the body's ability to fight off oral thrush and prevent recurrences.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh yarrow leaves and flowers, or use 1 tablespoon of dried yarrow, and rinse them thoroughly.
Crush the yarrow leaves and flowers gently to release their healing properties and place them in a teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent herb.
Drink the yarrow tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to soothe oral thrush symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them ideal for soothing oral thrush infections.
Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin and mouth infections, including oral thrush, due to their antiseptic properties.
Stems: Yarrow stems contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, making them useful for treating oral thrush symptoms.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are packed with antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and aid in the healing process of oral thrush.
Roots: Yarrow roots have antifungal properties that can help combat the Candida fungus responsible for oral thrush.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oral thrush with herbal teas is by using potent antifungal herbs such as oregano, goldenseal, and echinacea. These herbs can help to reduce the growth of candida fungus causing oral thrush. Steep one to two teaspoons of dried herbs in hot water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and drink the tea three to four times a day. Additionally, rinse your mouth with the tea for extra benefits. However, it's important to note that herbal teas should not replace medical treatment, especially if the infection is severe or persistent. Always consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oral thrush that are treated with herbal teas include candidiasis and other fungal infections in the mouth. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and echinacea have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of oral thrush, such as soreness, redness, and white patches in the mouth. These herbal teas can help soothe irritation, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system to fight off the infection. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas as a treatment for oral thrush or any other medical condition.
